sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Mama's Creations (MAMA) COO granted 3,500 RSUs vesting through 2029
feed.summary: Mama's Creations (MAMA) Chief Operating Officer, Tappan Moore III, has been granted 3,500 restricted stock units (RSUs) as part of a compensation award. These RSUs will vest in three equal annual installments on April 15, 2027, April 15, 2028, and April 15, 2029. Following this grant, Moore directly holds 13,341 shares of the company's common stock.

feed.title: Southwest pilots use evasive action to avoid collision at airport, company says
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines confirmed that pilots of Flight 507 took evasive action to avoid a collision with another Southwest flight at Nashville International Airport. While attempting a go-around due to gusty winds, Flight 507 was put on a path conflicting with Flight 1152, which was departing from a parallel runway, based on air traffic control instructions and onboard alerts. Both flight crews responded to their alerts, and no collision occurred, with both planes landing safely or continuing to their destinations.

feed.title: Textron Systems Awarded $9.5M Contract For Advanced EW Simulation To The USAF
feed.summary: Textron Systems Corporation has been awarded a $9.5 million contract to provide advanced electronic warfare (EW) simulators to the 68th Electronic Warfare Squadron at Eglin Air Force Base. These A2PATS simulators will be used by the USAF and allied nations to test the Eagle Passive Active Warning Survivability System (EPAWSS) sensor for F-15 upgrades and to train against modern EW threats in high-fidelity, secure environments. The contract has a 17-month performance period and includes support, testing, and spares for the deployed systems.

feed.title: SOUN stock rebounds after LivePerson deal: Retail bulls bank on AI opportunity with expanded Fortune 100 client base
feed.summary: SoundHound AI (SOUN) stock rebounded after announcing a strategic partnership with LivePerson (NASDAQ:LPSN), which will integrate SoundHound's AI voice assistant into LivePerson's customer engagement platform. This collaboration is expected to expand SoundHound's reach into the Fortune 100 client base and leverage the growing AI market, attracting retail investors. The deal builds on SoundHound's existing success in the automotive sector and is seen as a key step towards broader market penetration for its voice AI technology.

feed.title: Analysts Are Bullish on Top Technology Stocks: Motorola Solutions (MSI), Leidos Holdings (LDOS)
feed.summary: Bank of America Securities analysts have issued bullish ratings for Motorola Solutions (MSI) and Leidos Holdings (LDOS), alongside a maintained Buy rating for Apple (AAPL), indicating strong confidence in these technology stocks. Motorola Solutions received a Buy rating with a $500 price target, Leidos Holdings a Buy rating with a $200 price target, and Apple a Buy rating with a $325 price target. These ratings, backed by positive analyst consensus and significant upside potentials, suggest a favorable outlook for investors in the technology sector.

feed.title: Mizuho raises Prologis stock price target to $150 on growth outlook
feed.summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for Prologis (NYSE:PLD) stock to $150 from $143, maintaining an Outperform rating, following investor meetings and an update to their financial model based on strong first-quarter earnings. The firm projects 7% annual growth in Funds From Operations (FFO) for 2026 and 2027, driven by strategic capital funds and robust market conditions. Other analysts, including BofA Securities and BTIG, have also raised their price targets for Prologis, citing impressive leasing activity and improved occupancy rates.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Stock Jumps as Stifel Sets One of Street’s Highest Targets
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rose 3.47% after Stifel Nicolaus analyst Ruben Roy increased his price target to $320 from $280, citing strong AI compute demand and major commitments from Meta and OpenAI. This new target is one of the highest on Wall Street and implies a roughly 17% upside, driven by the belief that AMD's long-term EPS target of $20+ is a floor, not a ceiling. However, Stifel also warned about potential supply constraints as a key risk that could limit AMD's growth despite high demand.
